summary:
The facial nerve contains special visceral afferent fibers responsible for taste reception from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue. These sensory fibers synapse within the geniculate ganglion and project to the nucleus of the tractus solitarius.  

Final Answer: \boxed{C}

Only include information directly related to the anatomical location or function of the nerves involved; do not add commentary about other systems such as vision, hearing, motor functions, etc., unless they're part of the core argument regarding the specific query asked here.
